title: Reading Container Logs
description:
## Where Do Logs Come From?

For `docker logs <container>` to show your application's output, where should the application write its logs?

options.text: To a file inside the container, e.g. /var/log/app.log
To the container's **stdout and stderr** — Docker captures these streams via its logging driver
To the host's systemd journal directly

explanation: `docker logs` shows whatever the container's main process writes to **stdout/stderr**. Docker's logging driver (json-file by default) captures those streams. This is why the 12-factor convention is to log to stdout/stderr rather than to files — it lets the platform collect, rotate and ship logs uniformly. An app that writes only to a file inside the container will show nothing in `docker logs`.

hints.title: Follow logs live
hints.body: Use -f to stream and --tail to limit how many past lines you see.
